text.title: Code Interpreter Usage Rules
text.summary: Rules for attaching files to Code Interpreter, downloading generated outputs, and understanding sandbox constraints.

rule.statement: Attach files to the message, not the assistant, when the file is specific to one analysis task.
rule.rationale: Files attached to an assistant are available in all future threads. Files attached to a message are scoped to that interaction, limiting cost and data exposure.

rule.statement: Download Code Interpreter-generated image files immediately after the run completes; they expire.
rule.rationale: Generated images are stored as ephemeral image_file content blocks. If not downloaded promptly, the file_id becomes invalid and the image is lost.

section.title: Sandbox Constraints
section.p: Code Interpreter runs in an OpenAI-controlled sandbox. Custom package installation is not possible. External network access is not available from within the sandbox. Maximum file size limits apply per the API documentation.

rule.statement: Do not rely on Code Interpreter for tasks requiring custom library versions or external API calls from within executed code.
rule.rationale: The sandbox has a fixed package set and no outbound network. Tasks requiring custom dependencies must be handled outside the interpreter, with results passed back as file attachments.

example.note: Images are downloaded in the same loop that processes the response to minimize the risk of expiry.
